task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11
Classification: Internal leadership use only; not for client distribution

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios remains broadly ON TRACK, but the margin for delay has narrowed. Core architecture and the API gateway are complete, and the team is progressing toward the July integration/demo milestone. However, several near-term blockers now require active management: Meridian source data inconsistencies, GeoSync license renewal, incomplete test setup/UAT provisioning, dashboard performance validation, and a newly identified large-file ingestion failure.

The most important leadership message: the July 25 Meridian product demo is still achievable, but only if the clean Meridian sample dataset, GeoSync license, integration/UAT access, and performance benchmarking actions close on their current dates. UAT has already been extended by one week; any additional slippage of more than two weeks threatens the October 1 launch target and may trigger contract penalty review.

CURRENT PROGRESS
Overall health: ON TRACK with elevated execution risk.

Milestone and delivery status:
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Complete.
- System architecture design: Complete.
- Development environment setup: Complete.
- Data schema finalization: Complete.
- API gateway: Complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: Approximately 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: Approximately 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: Approximately 40% complete; target sign-off 2026-07-25.

Q2 carryover / overdue items:
- Integration test suite setup: Overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: Overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access, now expected by 2026-07-19.
- Performance benchmarking report: Overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor.

Sprint 14 testing status:
- Authentication flows: 4/4 passed.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passed; 2 high-severity failures remain open.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and 100k-record load-time benchmark are not yet complete.
- Overall Sprint 14 QA execution: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 failed, 3 not yet run.

Open defects:
- HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input. This affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data. Severity: High. Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion above approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes around the 210k-record mark. Severity: High. Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

KEY RISKS
1. Meridian source data quality / date format inconsistency
- Status: Active; highest combined likelihood and impact.
- Impact: Blocks clean ingestion, affects approximately 40% of source data, and threatens live-data demo readiness.
- Current mitigation: Meridian committed to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Leadership watchpoint: If dataset is late or incomplete, demo scope and credibility are at risk.

2. GeoSync license renewal pending
- Status: Active; legal review in progress.
- Impact: Could block mapping functionality required for the demo and downstream integration.
- Current target resolution: 2026-07-14.
- Leadership watchpoint: May require legal/procurement escalation if not closed by 2026-07-14.

3. Dashboard performance at scale
- Status: Active; benchmark task not yet started / not yet executed in QA.
- Impact: Meridian sponsor raised explicit concern about dashboard load time on large datasets. Q3 target is under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Current owner: Derek Okafor.
- Leadership watchpoint: Performance benchmarking must occur before the July 25 demo to avoid surprise failure in front of Meridian stakeholders.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Status: Active; UAT has already been extended by one week to 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Impact: More than two weeks of slippage could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Leadership watchpoint: Protect UAT entry criteria and environment readiness. Avoid introducing unapproved scope before UAT.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Status: Active.
- Impact: Blocks UAT environment provisioning and integration environment setup.
- Current dependencies: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT access expected 2026-07-19.
- Leadership watchpoint: If Meridian misses either date, NovaBridge should formally document dependency impact and adjust client-facing expectations.

6. Large-file ingestion memory overflow
- Status: Newly visible high-severity technical issue from Sprint 14 QA.
- Impact: Conflicts with Q3 ingestion throughput target of 500k records/min sustained and could undermine the live ingestion demo if Meridian sample data is large.
- Current owner: Amir Hassan.
- Leadership watchpoint: May need additional engineering support or architecture review if root cause is not isolated quickly.

7. Key person dependency
- Status: Monitored.
- Impact: Derek Okafor is carrying technical leadership, performance benchmarking, and demo-critical responsibilities; no backup tech lead is identified.
- Leadership watchpoint: Assign backup coverage before demo/UAT period.

8. Scope creep from Meridian feature requests
- Status: Monitored; three informal feature requests logged.
- Impact: Could consume capacity needed for integration, UAT readiness, and hardening.
- Leadership watchpoint: Enforce change control for proposed Q3 additions.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July 2026 — Complete system integration and demo readiness
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to provide IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- 2026-07-14: Resolve GeoSync license renewal.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to unblock date-format issue.
- 2026-07-18: Finalize data ingestion module.
- 2026-07-18: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access.
- 2026-07-20: Complete integration test suite setup.
- 2026-07-22: Complete client dashboard v1.
- 2026-07-25: Product demo for Meridian stakeholders and integration test sign-off target.

Product demo requirements for 2026-07-25:
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data; mock data will not satisfy sponsor expectations.
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Dashboard visualization with real data, aligned to contract Section 9.3.

August 2026 — UAT and feedback response
- UAT window: 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- NovaBridge to support Meridian user testing sessions.
- UAT feedback remediation budgeted for two sprints.

August–September 2026 — Performance hardening
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput benchmark target: 500k records/min sustained.
- External security penetration test: vendor TBD.

September 2026 — Launch preparation
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Target launch date: 2026-10-01.

Proposed Q3 feature additions requiring change control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs / cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor, deferred from Q2.

ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
1. Protect the July 25 demo
Leadership should request a daily demo-readiness checkpoint through 2026-07-25 covering: sample data, GeoSync license, large-file ingestion fix, dashboard benchmark, integration test status, and demo agenda readiness.

2. Escalate GeoSync license if not closed by 2026-07-14
Legal/procurement escalation may be needed to prevent a third-party dependency from affecting the Meridian demo.

3. Ensure Meridian dependency accountability
If Meridian misses the 2026-07-12 infrastructure diagram, 2026-07-15 clean sample data, or 2026-07-19 UAT access commitments, NovaBridge should document client-side dependency risk immediately and communicate schedule impact formally.

4. Add engineering support for ingestion stability and performance
The date normalization defect and large-file memory overflow both sit with the ingestion workstream. Leadership should consider adding senior engineering review/support to reduce risk before integration sign-off and UAT.

5. Assign backup technical leadership
No backup tech lead is currently identified for Derek Okafor. Given his ownership of performance benchmarking and demo-critical technical items, leadership should assign backup coverage before the July 25 demo and August UAT period.

6. Enforce Q3 change control
Meridian has made informal feature requests, and the roadmap includes proposed additions. These should not enter the committed scope without formal change control, schedule impact assessment, and commercial approval.

7. Monitor budget pressure
Q2 actuals exceeded budget, and Q3 pressure is expected from GeoSync licensing and the one-week UAT extension. CFO review is scheduled for 2026-07-18, and a budget reconciliation plan is due 2026-07-20. Leadership should align on whether to absorb, offset, or reduce scope/cost elsewhere, noting that internal overruns cannot be passed through to Meridian under the MSA.

RECOMMENDED LEADERSHIP ACTIONS THIS WEEK
- Confirm owner-level accountability for each demo-critical dependency and require daily status until 2026-07-25.
- Ask Legal/Procurement for a definitive GeoSync renewal path by 2026-07-14.
- Ask the PM to prepare a client-dependency impact note in case Meridian misses upcoming IT/data commitments.
- Assign a backup technical lead and additional engineering support for ingestion defects HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015.
- Require dashboard and ingestion performance benchmark results before the demo.
- Reconfirm that Q3 feature additions are deferred unless approved through formal change control.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios is still recoverable against the current Q3 plan and October 1 launch target, but the next two weeks are critical. The primary risk is not overall progress; it is convergence risk across demo-critical dependencies, test readiness, data quality, licensing, environment access, and performance validation. Leadership attention now should focus on fast dependency closure, technical risk reduction, and strict scope discipline.

content.text: Done. The comprehensive project health briefing has been saved as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
